sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER, value_list LIST<TINYINT>, char_val VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP, is_active BOOLEAN);

INSERT INTO test_data (id, value_list, char_val, created_at, is_active) VALUES (1, [1, 2, NULL], 'abc', '2023-10-01 10:00:00', TRUE), (2, [3, NULL, 4], 'def', '2023-10-01 10:05:00', FALSE), (3, [5, 6], 'ghi', '2023-10-01 10:10:00', TRUE);
SELECT id, array_has(value_list, 1) AS has_one FROM test_data WHERE array_has(value_list, 'abc'::TINYINT) IS TRUE;
SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE value_list <@ [1, 2, 3] AND char_val IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING array_has(value_list, 4) OR array_has(value_list, NULL);
SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY is_active ORDER BY created_at DESC) AS row_num FROM test_data WHERE (value_list <@ [1, 2]) AND (is_active = TRUE OR char_val IS NULL);
CREATE TABLE test_data_empty (id INTEGER, value_list LIST<TINYINT>);
SELECT id FROM test_data_empty WHERE value_list <@ [] AND array_has(value_list, NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data_empty (id, value_list) VALUES (1, [1]), (2, NULL), (3, []);
SELECT id FROM test_data_empty WHERE array_has(value_list, '1'::TINYINT) IS TRUE OR value_list <@ [1, 2];
